views.name: TonightView
views.purpose: Primary morning entry and daily summary. If today's sleep is not logged, shows a clean entry form: bedtime and wake time pickers displayed as large tappable time pills, a horizontal 1-5 star quality slider with haptic snap points, a horizontal mood emoji selector (5 mood icons in a row — tappable with selection ring), dream recall toggle, and optional notes field. Once logged, transforms into a summary card showing a circular sleep score ring (0-100, gradient from red through yellow to green), computed sleep duration in large text, quality stars, mood icon, and a small 'Edit' button to re-enter edit mode. The sleep score ring animates on appear.

views.name: WeekView
views.purpose: Weekly sleep pattern visualization. Shows a 7-day vertical timeline chart where each row is a day (Mon-Sun), and each bar stretches horizontally from bedtime to wake time (e.g., 11pm to 7am), positioned against a time axis. Bars are color-coded by quality rating (muted red for 1, vibrant green for 5). Vertical dashed line marks the user's sleep goal target (8h). Below the chart: a stats row with weekly average hours, average bedtime, average wake time, and bedtime consistency (standard deviation). Tapping any day bar expands an inline detail card showing that night's full entry (duration, quality, mood, notes). Empty days show a ghost bar with 'No entry' label.

views.name: TrendsView
views.purpose: Long-term sleep analytics and actionable insights. Sections from top: (1) Sleep Debt gauge — a horizontal progress bar showing cumulative hours below target over past 14 days, colored green (<2h debt) to red (>8h debt) with numeric label. (2) Consistency Score — circular badge 0-100 based on bedtime standard deviation over 30 days, with verbal label (Excellent/Good/Fair/Poor). (3) 30-day duration trend — sparkline chart of nightly hours with horizontal 8h target line, dots colored by quality. (4) Mood breakdown — horizontal stacked bar showing distribution of wake moods over past 30 days, each segment colored by mood (gold for energized, green for refreshed, gray for neutral, blue for groggy, red for exhausted). (5) Insights cards — dynamically generated text insights like 'You sleep 45min more on weekends' or 'Higher quality nights correlate with earlier bedtimes'. (6) Streak counter — flame icon with consecutive logging days.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: sleepDuration
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Computes hours between bedTime and wakeTime for a given entry. Handles overnight spans (bedTime > wakeTime means crossing midnight). Returns value in decimal hours (e.g., 7.5).

viewModelComputedProperties.name: sleepScore
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Composite 0-100 score. 40% duration component: 100 if duration equals sleepGoal, loses 12.5 points per hour deviation (linear). 40% quality component: qualityRating mapped from 1-5 to 0-100. 20% consistency component: uses bedtime deviation from 30-day average, 100 if within 15min, 0 if >90min, linear between. Clamped to 0-100.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: todayEntry
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Returns the sleep entry logged for today's date, or nil if not yet logged. Used by TonightView to toggle between entry form and summary display.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: currentStreak
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Consecutive calendar days with a sleep entry, counting backwards from today (or yesterday if today not yet logged). Breaks on first missing day.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: weeklyAverage
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Mean sleep duration in hours over the past 7 calendar days. Only counts days with entries. Returns 0 if no entries.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: sleepDebt
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Sum of (sleepGoal - actualDuration) for each of past 14 days where duration < goal. Always >= 0. Days without entries count as full goal deficit.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: consistencyScore
viewModelComputedProperties.description: 0-100 score from bedtime regularity over past 30 days. Computes standard deviation of bedtime (minutes from midnight). stdev <= 15min = 100, >= 90min = 0, linear interpolation between. Requires >= 5 entries or returns 0.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: averageBedtime
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Mean bedtime over past 30 days. Converts each bedTime to minutes-from-midnight (handling pre/post midnight), averages, converts back to a Date with only hour/minute components. Nil if no entries.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: weekEntries
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Past 7 calendar days of sleep entries, sorted oldest to newest. Includes days without entries as gaps.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: moodDistribution
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Count of each WakeMood value over past 30 days. Used by TrendsView mood stacked bar.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: weekdayVsWeekendAverage
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Average sleep hours split by weekday (Mon-Fri) vs weekend (Sat-Sun) over past 30 days. Used for weekend oversleep insight.

dailyGoal.description: Target sleep hours per night. User-adjustable in a settings section within TrendsView.

domainLogic.streakDefinition: A day counts toward the streak if a sleep entry exists for that calendar date, regardless of duration or quality values.
domainLogic.trendAnalysis: Primary trends: (1) Weekly average hours compared to previous week (show delta with up/down arrow). (2) Bedtime consistency as 30-day rolling standard deviation in minutes. (3) Sleep debt as 14-day rolling cumulative deficit. (4) Quality trend as 7-day moving average of qualityRating. Thresholds: sleep debt > 5h triggers alert card, consistency stdev > 60min triggers 'irregular schedule' warning.
insights: Sleep debt alert: 'You have Xh of sleep debt over the past 2 weeks. Consider an earlier bedtime tonight.' when debt > 5h
Weekend oversleep: 'You sleep X min more on weekends vs weekdays' when weekend avg exceeds weekday avg by > 30min
Quality-duration correlation: Pearson r between duration and qualityRating over 30 days. If r > 0.5: 'Longer sleep strongly improves your quality rating'
Best bedtime window: Find the bedtime hour range that produces the highest average quality rating. 'Your best sleep quality happens when you go to bed between Xpm and Ypm'
Mood-duration link: Which duration range (buckets: <6h, 6-7h, 7-8h, 8-9h, 9h+) produces the most energized/refreshed moods
Consistency streak: 'You kept a consistent bedtime for X days in a row!' when bedtime stays within 30min of average for 7+ consecutive entries
